_note: Chapter 3 — Marly hired by Virek. Brussels interview -> the garden construct -> Paris base.

c3_gallery.name: Galerie Duperey — Interview
c3_gallery.desc: Glass, steel, and money too old to raise its voice. The receptionist's smile is exactly four percent warm. You are one scandal deep and they made you wait forty minutes to be sure you felt it.

c3_construct.name: Virek's Garden — Parque Güell
c3_construct.desc: A sensory link, and then Barcelona: Gaudí's serpentine bench, tiled dragons, sun that has been dead for hours falling warm on your arms. Josef Virek sits on the bench feeding pigeons that don't exist. His body is a shed of support vats in Stockholm. This is where he lives.
c3_construct.locked_text: The link isn't open to you yet. The interview comes first.

c3_street.name: Brussels — Rue au Beurre, Rain
c3_street.desc: Grey rain on grey stone, your best coat soaked past saving. In a shop window, thirty screens replay a simstim star's smile. Every taxi in the city is somewhere else.

c3_train.name: TGV — Brussels to Paris
c3_train.desc: The flat country pours past at three hundred kph. In the seatback screen your own face looks out from an art-world scandal retrospective, four months old and still warm. Paris will know you before you arrive. Paris always did.

c3_ternes.name: Quartier des Ternes
c3_ternes.desc: Andrea's street: chestnut trees, dog-eared bookshops, an old man selling roasted things from a cart. The city that ruined you, smelling like the only home you've got.

c3_andrea.name: Andrea's Flat
c3_andrea.desc: Books on every surface, a couch that knows you, coffee in mismatched cups. Andrea listens the way nobody in the gallery world ever has: to find out what's true.

c3_cafe.name: Café Blanc
c3_cafe.desc: Marble tables, bitter espresso, a terminal by the register older than the waiter. From the corner seat you can watch the whole street — which matters now, because the whole street is watching you back.
